summary: Iter 109 (Employee Master extras + drafts + review modal + PDF) — ALL PASS. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests green (create with pay_mode='Cash', compliance salary allow/ded, permanent_address, emergency_contact, 2 family_members persisted & returned by GET profile; PATCH switches pay_mode->'Bank', replaces deductions, resizes family list; master-pdf 200 + pypdf extract confirms 'Designation','SALARY DETAILS','Pay Mode','FAMILY DETAILS','Emergency','Permanent','PF No','ESI IP' and family member name; drafts CRUD upsert + list + delete + company-admin scope 403 for foreign firm). Frontend (desktop 1920, super admin, firm locked to cmp_527fecdd7c): /employee-add shows Present Address, Permanent Address, Emergency Contact Name/No., Family Details section; testID family-add adds a row (del-0 appears), testID family-del-1 removes rows; Save as Draft (save-draft-btn) fires alert 'Draft saved ✓', draft-list persists after reload, draft-resume-* refills name field, draft-del-* deletes; Create employee opens 'Check details before creating' modal listing Pay Mode/Permanent Address/Family Members/Emergency; review-back closes modal, review-confirm renders with all fields. No React Native / Expo issues detected.
critical_code_review_comments: Note: Console logs show harmless RN-web style warnings ('shadow*' deprecated, 'outline'/'background' style props). Pre-existing.
Draft flow: alert() blocks the JS thread; on web the message uses ✓ emoji — fine for desktop portal.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 employee then deleted via DELETE /api/admin/employees/{id} (cleanup passed). Created 2 drafts and deleted both. No residual test artefacts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 109 fully verified. Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter109_employee_master.py (10 tests, ~2.5s). For UI: log in via POST /api/auth/admin-password-login -> put token in localStorage keys 'llc_session_token' AND 'auth:token', set 'selected_company_id'='cmp_527fecdd7c', navigate to /employee-add and select the firm from the top ADD-UNDER-FIRM native <select> (else the whole form stays disabled — this caught me on first run). Alerts appear via native window.alert on web (register page.on('dialog', ...) BEFORE the click).
